purpose: Valibot is a modular, type-safe schema library for TypeScript/JavaScript used to validate and transform unknown data at runtime (servers, forms, config files). It uses many small, independent, tree-shakable functions to describe schemas, pipelines (validations/transformations), and infer static types, enabling bundle sizes starting under 700 bytes.

public_api.name: object
public_api.summary: Creates an object schema from a record of property schemas.

public_api.name: string
public_api.summary: Schema for string values.

public_api.name: number
public_api.summary: Schema for number values.

public_api.name: boolean
public_api.summary: Schema for boolean values.

public_api.name: array
public_api.summary: Schema for arrays with a given item schema.

public_api.name: pipe
public_api.summary: Composes a schema with validation/transformation actions.

public_api.name: email
public_api.summary: Pipeline action validating an email string.

public_api.name: minLength
public_api.summary: Pipeline action requiring a minimum length.

public_api.name: parse
public_api.summary: Parses input against schema; throws on failure.

public_api.name: safeParse
public_api.summary: Parses input and returns a result object instead of throwing.

public_api.name: is
public_api.summary: Type guard that returns a boolean indicating whether input matches schema.

public_api.name: InferOutput
public_api.summary: Utility type inferring the output TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: InferInput
public_api.summary: Utility type inferring the input TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: parser
public_api.summary: Creates a reusable parser function for a schema.

public_api.name: safeParser
public_api.summary: Creates a reusable safe parser function for a schema.

public_api.name: parseAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of parse for schemas containing async actions.

public_api.name: safeParseAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of safeParse.

public_api.name: objectAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of object for async entry schemas.

public_api.name: pipeAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of pipe supporting async actions.

public_api.name: optional
public_api.summary: Marks a schema as optional (may be undefined).

public_api.name: nullable
public_api.summary: Allows null in addition to the wrapped schema.

public_api.name: nullish
public_api.summary: Allows null and undefined in addition to wrapped schema.

public_api.name: union
public_api.summary: Schema matching one of the provided option schemas.

public_api.name: intersect
public_api.summary: Schema matching the intersection of provided schemas.

public_api.name: literal
public_api.summary: Schema matching a literal value.

public_api.name: picklist
public_api.summary: Schema matching one of a list of allowed values.

public_api.name: enum
public_api.summary: Schema validating values of a TypeScript enum.

public_api.name: record
public_api.summary: Schema for records with given key and value schemas.

public_api.name: tuple
public_api.summary: Schema for fixed-shape tuples.

public_api.name: transform
public_api.summary: Pipeline action that transforms the value.

public_api.name: check
public_api.summary: Pipeline action running a custom boolean check.

public_api.name: regex
public_api.summary: Pipeline action validating string against a regex.

public_api.name: url
public_api.summary: Pipeline action validating a URL string.

public_api.name: uuid
public_api.summary: Pipeline action validating a UUID string.

public_api.name: flatten
public_api.summary: Flattens issues into nested/root error maps.

public_api.name: ValiError
public_api.summary: Error class thrown by parse on validation failure.

public_api.name: isValiError
public_api.summary: Type guard identifying ValiError instances.

public_api.name: getDotPath
public_api.summary: Utility returning the dot path of an issue.

usage_patterns.title: Define a schema and infer its type
usage_patterns.body: Build a schema with v.object plus per-field schemas composed via v.pipe; use v.InferOutput<typeof Schema> to get the static TypeScript type. The schema doubles as a runtime validator and a type definition.

usage_patterns.title: Validate data at runtime
usage_patterns.body: Use v.parse(Schema, input) to validate and get typed output, throwing on failure. Prefer v.safeParse for a non-exception result object, or v.is as a boolean type guard when you just need to narrow a type.

usage_patterns.title: Tree-shakable modular imports
usage_patterns.body: Import as `import * as v from 'valibot'` or cherry-pick named exports so bundlers can drop unused code. Each validator, transformer, and schema is an independent function, keeping bundle size minimal (starting under 700 bytes).

usage_patterns.title: Async validation
usage_patterns.body: For async rules or schemas, use *Async counterparts such as objectAsync, pipeAsync, parseAsync, safeParseAsync. Mix them where async actions are needed; sync schemas can still be used with async parsers.

gotchas: parse throws a ValiError on failure; use safeParse or is when you want to avoid exceptions.
Validations and transformations are applied via v.pipe(schema, ...actions), not as chained methods on the schema.
Async actions require *Async schemas/parsers (e.g., parseAsync, pipeAsync, objectAsync); using plain parse with async actions will not work.
Valibot's API differs from Zod: migrating requires using the dedicated migration guide rather than direct method mapping.

examples.title: Login schema with parse and type inference
